_meta.purpose: Source-of-truth capability map. Each capability_id is the join key for proposal authoring (Vol 2 §1, §2, §3, §6 + Vol 5 §5.2 + cost-volume scoping). Templates pull capability paragraphs and PI bio paragraphs by capability_id.
_meta.review_cycle: Update on each Phase I/II submission. Annual full review.

sdvosb.verified_in_sam: [CONFIRM: David — VetCert flow-through to SAM.gov visible by 2026-05-01? Set status here.]

capabilities.one_line: ARTCC simulation environment with live ADS-B integration for air traffic control workforce training and airspace-operations research.

trl.current: [CONFIRM: David — 3 or 4? Working sim with live ADS-B = component validated in lab → 3. If integrated subsystem demonstrated against operational sim conditions → 4.]
evidence_for_current_trl: [CONFIRM: David — list working components: OpenSky-based ARTCC sim, ADS-B feed integration, sector handoff logic, etc.]
[CONFIRM: David — what's NOT yet done: CI/tests, formal eval against published baseline, scenario library size]

prior_work_artifacts: [CONFIRM: David — list demonstrable artifacts: code repo URL, demo video URL, internal test reports, screencasts]
exclusions: Vector is not a certified-simulator product; no FAA Part 142 or DOD aircrew certification claims.
capabilities.narrative_paragraph: [NEEDS DAVID INPUT: 2-paragraph Vector capability description in your voice. Skeleton: Paragraph 1 = what Vector is today (ARTCC simulation with live ADS-B), what it does that nothing else does, the technical mechanism. Paragraph 2 = what it lets a researcher or training program do that they couldn't do before, with one concrete example. No banned words.]

capabilities.one_line: 21-agent autonomous fleet for software development with multi-agent coordination, observability, and approval-pipeline gates.

trl.current: [CONFIRM: David — 4 or 5? Operating fleet = at minimum subsystem demonstration in relevant environment.]
evidence_for_current_trl: [CONFIRM: David — operational metrics: # of agents in active use, throughput stats, observability dashboard URL]

prior_work_artifacts: [CONFIRM: David — komatik-agents repo URL, Base Camp dashboard URL, any published case studies]
phase3_target_customers: AFRL/RI agentic-systems programs (ASIMOV, ANSR, KAIROS lineage)
exclusions: Not a general-purpose LLM platform; not a model-training shop; not an inference-infrastructure provider.
capabilities.narrative_paragraph: [NEEDS DAVID INPUT: 2-paragraph Base Camp capability description in your voice.]

capabilities.one_line: Audit-bundle tooling layer for AI and software systems. Builds defender-actionable audit graphs from system implementation, runtime behavior, and supply-chain provenance.

trl.current: [CONFIRM: David — 2 or 3? If concept validated on a specific system, TRL 3. If only methodology paper / design, TRL 2.]
evidence_for_current_trl: [CONFIRM: David — what Trace has actually produced as artifacts vs. what's still on the methodology drawing board]

prior_work_artifacts: [CONFIRM: David — list any Trace-generated audit bundles, methodology docs, code repo if open]
phase3_target_customers: AI governance buyers (HHS, ED, civilian agencies)
exclusions: Trace is not a SAST/DAST replacement; not a SIEM; not a vulnerability scanner. It is a layer above those tools producing audit-actionable artifacts.
capabilities.narrative_paragraph: [NEEDS DAVID INPUT: 2-paragraph Trace capability description in your voice. KEY: do not anchor on cryptographic-protocol analysis (this killed ASEMA). Anchor on audit-graph methodology over implementation/runtime/supply-chain.]

_archive_capabilities._note: Capabilities mentioned in V4.1 brand line that are not currently positioned for SBIR/grant work. Listed for completeness; not joined to active proposal templates.
